# mdns2hosts — LLM Context

## What this program does

mdns2hosts is a Windows CLI tool that resolves mDNS `.local` names via multicast DNS queries and syncs their IPv4 addresses into the Windows hosts file. It manages a delimited block in the hosts file and never modifies entries outside that block.

## Repository structure

```
cmd/           CLI commands (cobra)
  root.go      Root command
  sync.go      sync <name...>  — resolve mDNS names, write to hosts
  watch.go     watch <name...> — poll mDNS, update hosts on IP change
  clean.go     clean           — remove managed block content
  install.go   install-service — register as Windows service (needs Admin)
  uninstall.go uninstall-service — stop & remove Windows service
  service_run.go  hidden entry point for SCM (Windows service)
mdns/          mDNS resolver (raw multicast A-record queries)
hosts/         Hosts file reader/writer (managed block, atomic writes)
service/       Windows service integration (build tag: windows)
main.go        Entry point
```

## Building

```bash
# On Windows
go build -o mdns2hosts.exe .

# Cross-compile from macOS/Linux
GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build -o mdns2hosts.exe .
```

Requires Go 1.24+. Dependencies: `github.com/spf13/cobra`, `github.com/miekg/dns`, `golang.org/x/sys`.

## How to install for a user

1. Download or build `mdns2hosts.exe`.
2. Place it somewhere on `PATH`, e.g. `C:\tools\mdns2hosts.exe`.
3. Run from an elevated (Administrator) terminal — writing to the hosts file requires elevation.

## CLI reference

| Command | Args | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sync` | `<name...>` | Resolve names via mDNS, write IPs to hosts managed block |
| `watch` | `<name...>` | Poll mDNS on interval, update hosts on IP changes. Flags: `--interval` (default 30s) |
| `clean` | (none) | Remove content between managed block markers |
| `install-service` | (none) | Register as Windows service. Flags: `--name` (required), `--interval` (default 30s) |
| `uninstall-service` | (none) | Stop and unregister Windows service |

## What the hosts file looks like

The tool manages only the block between these markers:

```
# BEGIN mdns2hosts
192.168.2.179 supercow.local
# END mdns2hosts
```

If the markers don't exist on first run, they are appended. Other entries in the hosts file are preserved exactly, including comments, whitespace, and line endings.

## How the mDNS resolver works

1. Enumerate all network interfaces that are UP and support multicast.
2. For each interface: open a UDP multicast listener on `224.0.0.251:5353`.
3. Send an A-record query for `<name>.local.`.
4. Wait for responses (up to 3s timeout per interface).
5. Return the first matching IPv4 address found.

This bypasses system DNS and the hosts file — it queries mDNS directly on the wire.

## How the hosts manager works

- `ReadHosts()` parses the hosts file into three sections: before-block, block entries, after-block.
- `WriteHosts()` reassembles the file, writing the block entries sorted by hostname.
- Writes go to a temp file first, then `os.Rename` — atomic on NTFS.
- Line endings are always `\r\n` (CRLF).
- `EnsureBlock()` appends markers if they don't exist yet.

## How the Windows service works

- `install-service` saves a config file (`mdns2hosts.conf`) next to the binary with `NAMES=...` and `INTERVAL=...`.
- Registers with the Windows Service Control Manager (SCM) as `mdns2hosts`, start type = Automatic.
- When SCM starts the service, it runs `mdns2hosts.exe service-run`, which executes the handler loop.
- The handler polls mDNS on the configured interval and updates the hosts file via event log.

## Platform support

- **Windows only** for service mode (`service_run.go`, `service_windows.go`, `handler_windows.go` — build tag `windows`).
- **Cross-platform** for CLI commands (`sync`, `watch`, `clean`) — they work anywhere Go supports multicast UDP.
- Non-Windows builds have stubs for `Install`, `Uninstall`, `RunService` that return errors.

## Testing

```bash
go test ./... -cover
```

Test files: `mdns/resolver_test.go`, `hosts/manager_test.go`, `service/service_test.go`.

## Known constraints

- IPv4 only. IPv6 mDNS results are ignored.
- The hosts file path uses `SystemRoot` env var, defaulting to `C:\Windows`.
- `watch` polls at an interval — it does not use mDNS push notifications.
- Service mode writes to the Windows event log. If the event log source isn't registered, logs go to stderr.
